summary:
House Joint Resolution 42 aims to disapprove and block a specific local law enacted by the District of Columbia Council known as the Comprehensive Policing and Justice Reform Amendment Act of 2022 (D.C. Act 24-781). The resolution exercises Congress's authority under section 602(c)(1) of the District of Columbia Home Rule Act, which allows Congress to review and reject laws passed by the D.C. government. By enacting this joint resolution, Congress formally disapproves of the D.C. Council's action in approving the policing and justice reform measure, which the Council enacted on January 19, 2023, and transmitted to Congress on January 26, 2023. 

The text of the resolution does not detail the specific provisions of the D.C. law it seeks to block, nor does it specify an effective date beyond the standard legislative process. As a joint resolution, it requires passage by both the House of Representatives and the Senate, followed by the President's signature, to take effect. The resolution passed the House of Representatives on April 19, 2023. It was subsequently received in the Senate on April 20, 2023, read twice, and referred to the Senate Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs for further consideration.
